title: View dashboard metrics and goal status summary while authenticated
description: Verifies an authenticated user can load the dashboard and see trend metrics and goal status counts displayed.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the dashboard
Verify dashboard metrics and trend data are displayed
Verify goal status counts summary is displayed

title: Fallback to cached dashboard metrics when online metrics are unavailable
description: Verifies the dashboard can display cached metrics from local storage when online metrics are unavailable and still renders trend data.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the dashboard
Toggle the application to offline or local mode from the UI controls
Verify dashboard metrics are displayed using locally available cached data
Verify the trend chart is displayed

title: Start a focus session, complete as not achieved, and create a linked failure
description: Verifies the user can start a session for a goal, complete it as not achieved, and record a failure linked to that session and goal.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open goal tracking
Start creating a new goal
Fill Goal title with Session Goal
Select the goal period as Daily
Save the goal
Click the navigation item to open the session timer
Select the goal Session Goal for the session
Select a valid work duration
Select a valid rest duration
Start the focus session
Complete the session marking achieved as false
Fill in mistakes with a valid entry
Fill in improvements with a valid entry
Submit the session completion
Create a failure linked to the completed session
Fill Failure title with Session failure
Fill in failure notes with a valid entry
Save the failure
Verify the failure is created and visible in the failures list
Verify the failure shows a link to its associated session or goal

title: Soft-delete a goal to Trash then restore it
description: User soft-deletes a newly created goal, sees it in Trash, restores it, and the goal returns to the goals list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Goals
Create a new goal with name Goal C
Delete Goal C
Click the navigation link to Trash
Open the trashed item corresponding to Goal C
Restore the trashed item
Click the navigation link to Goals
Verify Goal C is displayed in the goals list

title: Create a yearly goal with a monthly child and view progress breakdown
description: Verifies the user can create a parent goal and a child goal and then view a progress breakdown that includes child goals.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open goal tracking
Start creating a new goal
Fill Goal title with 2026 Finance
Select the goal period as Yearly
Select the goal category as Finance
Save the goal
Open the newly created goal details
Start creating a child goal
Fill Goal title with March Budget
Select the goal period as Monthly
Save the goal
Open the parent goal progress view
Verify a progress breakdown is displayed and includes child goal contributions
Verify the parent goal shows an overall progress value

title: Create weekly life journal entry with linked goal and failure then filter weekly
description: User creates a weekly life journal entry linked to a newly created goal and failure and then filters journals by weekly scope to see the new entry in the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Goals
Create a new goal with name Goal A
Click the navigation link to Failures
Create a new failure with title Failure A and link it to Goal A
Click the navigation link to Journal
Start creating a new journal entry
Select weekly as the journal scope
Select life as the journal type
Fill Reflections for Goal A into the content field
Link the journal entry to Goal A
Link the journal entry to Failure A
Save the journal entry
Filter the journals list by weekly scope
Verify the newly created journal entry is displayed in the journals list

title: Update goal status or progress and reflect changes in dashboard counts
description: Verifies updating a goal status/progress is persisted and the dashboard status counts reflect the update.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open goal tracking
Start creating a new goal
Fill Goal title with Status Count Seed Goal
Select the goal period as Weekly
Save the goal
Open the goal details
Change the goal status to a different valid status
Save the goal changes
Click the navigation item to open the dashboard
Verify goal status counts summary reflects the updated goal status
Verify dashboard metrics remain displayed

title: Create failure linked to an existing goal and verify it appears in failures list
description: User creates a failure linked to a goal and then sees the failure in the failures list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Goals
Create a new goal with name Goal B
Click the navigation link to Failures
Start creating a new failure
Fill Missed focus into the title field
Fill Got distracted into the notes field
Link the failure to Goal B
Save the failure
Verify the newly created failure is displayed in the failures list

title: Switch dashboard range and update trend chart
description: Verifies the user can change the dashboard time range and the trend visualization updates accordingly.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the dashboard
Select a different time range for dashboard metrics
Verify the trend chart reflects the newly selected range
Verify the dashboard metrics summary remains displayed

title: Permanently delete a trashed journal so it cannot be restored
description: User deletes a journal to Trash, permanently deletes it from Trash, and the restore action is no longer possible because the item is removed from Trash.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Journal
Start creating a new journal entry
Select weekly as the journal scope
Select life as the journal type
Fill Entry to delete into the content field
Save the journal entry
Delete the newly created journal entry
Click the navigation link to Trash
Open the trashed item corresponding to the deleted journal entry
Permanently delete the trashed item
Verify the deleted journal item is no longer displayed in the Trash list

title: Block starting a second active focus session
description: Verifies the UI enforces only one active session by preventing a second session from starting while one is active.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open goal tracking
Start creating a new goal
Fill Goal title with Goal A
Select the goal period as Daily
Save the goal
Start creating a new goal
Fill Goal title with Goal B
Select the goal period as Daily
Save the goal
Click the navigation item to open the session timer
Select the goal Goal A for the session
Select a valid work duration
Select a valid rest duration
Start the focus session
Attempt to start another session for goal Goal B
Verify the UI indicates an active session already exists and blocks the second start
Verify only one session remains active

title: Render dashboard trends with no available data
description: Verifies the dashboard handles an empty dataset by showing an appropriate empty state while keeping the page usable.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open the dashboard
Select a time range that results in no recorded activity
Verify an empty state for trend data is displayed
Verify goal status counts summary is still displayed

title: Validate required fields when creating a goal
description: Verifies the app prevents saving a goal without required fields and shows a validation error.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in the email field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item to open goal tracking
Start creating a new goal
Select the goal period as Monthly
Submit the goal creation form
Verify a validation error is visible for missing required fields
Verify the goal is not added to the goals list

title: Block saving a failure when linked session is invalid or unavailable
description: User cannot save a failure when it is linked to a session that is not available in the UI and an error is shown.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Failures
Start creating a new failure
Fill Linked to non-existent session into the title field
Attempt to link the failure to a session using an invalid session reference in the session field
Attempt to save the failure
Verify a linking or not-found error is visible and the failure is not created

title: Prevent creating journal entry without required content
description: User cannot save a journal entry when required content is missing and a validation error is shown.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill {{LOGIN_USER}} into the email field
Fill {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the login form
Click the navigation link to Journal
Start creating a new journal entry
Select goal as the journal type
Attempt to save the journal entry without filling the content field
Verify a content validation error is visible
